raw_text:
EXECUTIVE SUMMARY
Total spend for November 2024 came in at $627,900 against a budget of $550,000, representing an overrun of $77,900 (+14.2%). The variance is driven primarily by people-cost pressures across all five salary lines and a near-doubling of Contractor Spend, the latter tied to an approved platform migration project.
The key drivers are:
- **Engineering Salaries** (+$18,000): A new Staff Engineer hire (Priya Sharma) onboarded one week ahead of the planned November start date, pulling her first full payroll into November and increasing Engineering headcount to 12 vs. a plan of 11. No further unplanned hires are expected before year-end.
- **Product Salaries** (+$12,000): A one-month overlap between an outgoing Product Manager (Marcus, serving notice through 30 November) and his replacement (Sarah, who started 1 November) resulted in a temporary double-salary position. This is expected to normalise in December.
- **Marketing Salaries** (+$8,400): Q4 performance bonuses totalling $8,400 were paid on 15 November and posted to the salaries line rather than a dedicated bonus budget line. This represents a coding issue that warrants reclassification.
- **Operations Salaries** (+$7,000): Five temporary agency staff were added for peak-season warehouse support in November and December, at an incremental cost of $7,000 per month. The spend was discussed in an operations budget review but was not reflected in the original headcount plan.
- **G&A Salaries** (+$4,000): A finance contractor (Clara Mendez) was engaged for three months of Q4 close support at $4,000 per month, posted to G&A Salaries per CFO instruction. This cost is likely to recur through January.
- **Contractor Spend** (+$18,500): Two senior contractors (TechForce Ltd — DevOps; NovaBuild Inc — Backend) were engaged from 28 October under a CTO/CFO co-signed SOW for an 8-week platform migration. A third specialist (AWS migration) was added in week three at an incremental $6,500, bringing total November spend to $18,500 against a $20,000 budget. The $18,500 is confirmed within the $50,000 total project budget (PLAT-001, Milestone 2 of 4 complete).
Across the salary lines, the overage pattern — consistent 13–17% across five departments — suggests that multiple concurrent headcount timing events and off-cycle payments, rather than any single structural shift, are the primary cause. Finance should assess whether the October budget model reflected the approved headcount plan accurately, and whether a budget reforecast is warranted for December through year-end given the ongoing Operations and G&A additions.

LINE COMMENTARY

Engineering Salaries | +$18,000 (+15.0%) | Budget: $120,000 | Actual: $138,000
The $18,000 overage is primarily driven by the early onboarding of Priya Sharma (Staff Engineer, L5, base salary $185,000 annualised). Her offer letter confirmed a start date of 28 October — one week ahead of the originally planned 4 November date — meaning her first full payroll cycle fell entirely within November. This increased Engineering headcount to 12 versus the budgeted plan of 11. Slack confirms no further unplanned hires are expected before year-end, suggesting the run-rate overage from this hire should be partially anticipated in future monthly budgets. The precise dollar contribution of the early start versus a full month's salary at the incremented headcount level has not been fully decomposed in the available evidence — a payroll reconciliation is recommended to confirm the split.
Sources
- Gmail - 2024-11-20T18:15:00Z - gmail-engineering_salaries-broad - Offer letter to Priya Sharma — start date 28 October per her request. Original plan was 4 November. First full payroll: November.
- Slack - 2024-11-20T12:15:00Z - slack-engineering_salaries-broad - Priya Sharma (Staff Engineer, L5) started 28 October — first full salary run hits November. Comp: $185K base. Previous plan assumed November start but she onboarded a week early, creating one extra pay period overlap.
- Slack - 2024-11-20T12:45:00Z - slack-engineering_salaries-narrow - Engineering headcount now 12 vs plan of 11. No further planned hires before year-end.

Product Salaries | +$12,000 (+15.0%) | Budget: $80,000 | Actual: $92,000
The $12,000 overage reflects a temporary double-salary position in November. Sarah (incoming Product Manager) commenced on 1 November, while Marcus (outgoing Product Manager) served his notice period through 30 November, resulting in both individuals being on payroll simultaneously for the full month. This is a one-month timing event; the position is expected to normalise to a single salary in December once Marcus's exit is processed. No formal Gmail approval was found for this overlap — finance should confirm whether the notice period terms were captured in Marcus's exit documentation.
Sources
- Slack - 2024-11-16T12:15:00Z - slack-product_salaries-broad - Note on Product: Marcus (outgoing PM) is serving his notice through 30 November. Sarah (incoming PM) started 1 November. Double salary in November only — Marcus exits end of month.

Marketing Salaries | +$8,400 (+14.0%) | Budget: $60,000 | Actual: $68,400
The entire $8,400 variance is attributable to Q4 performance bonuses paid on 15 November to the Marketing team, based on approved Q3 performance review outcomes. Notably, these payments were posted to the Marketing Salaries budget line rather than a dedicated bonuses line. The Slack evidence explicitly flags this as a coding issue. A reclassification entry is recommended to move the $8,400 to the appropriate bonus or variable compensation line, which would bring Marketing Salaries back in line with budget. Finance should also verify whether the bonus payments had formal written approval beyond the Slack reference.
Sources
- Slack - 2024-11-18T12:15:00Z - slack-marketing_salaries-broad - Q4 performance bonuses paid 15 November for Marketing team. $8,400 total across the team per the approved Q3 performance review outcomes. Separate from base salary line — should have been budgeted under bonuses but posted to salaries.

Operations Salaries | +$7,000 (+17.5%) | Budget: $40,000 | Actual: $47,000
The $7,000 overage represents the cost of five temporary agency warehouse staff added at the start of November to support peak-season operations. At $7,000 per month, this cost is expected to continue in December. The Slack record notes the spend was discussed during an operations budget review but was not reflected in the original headcount budget. Finance should confirm whether a formal budget amendment was raised following that review, and whether December's budget has been updated to capture the anticipated $7,000 recurring cost. No Gmail approval for the agency engagement was found — an invoice or PO confirmation is recommended.
Sources
- Slack - 2024-11-19T12:15:00Z - slack-operations_salaries-broad - Peak season starts — 5 temporary warehouse staff added for November and December. Bringing in via agency, posting to Ops Salaries. Cost: $7,000 additional per month. Planned in operations budget review, not reflected in original headcount plan.

G&A Salaries | +$4,000 (+13.3%) | Budget: $30,000 | Actual: $34,000
The $4,000 variance reflects the first month of a three-month engagement with finance contractor Clara Mendez, brought in for Q4 year-end close support. Per CFO instruction, the cost is posted to G&A Salaries rather than Contractor Spend. At $4,000 per month, this cost may continue through January 2025. Finance should note that the posting treatment — to Salaries rather than Contractor Spend — means this engagement is not visible in the Contractor Spend line and may distort comparisons in both categories. A consistent posting policy for contractors should be confirmed for December and January.
Sources
- Slack - 2024-11-12T12:15:00Z - slack-g&a_salaries-broad - Brought in Clara Mendez (finance contractor) for Q4 close support. $4,000/month, posting to G&A Salaries per CFO instruction. Year-end close support, 3-month engagement.

Contractor Spend | +$18,500 (+92.5%) | Budget: $20,000 | Actual: $38,500
The $18,500 overage (against a $20,000 budget) is driven by three contractors engaged for a platform migration project (CRM ref: PLAT-001). Two contractors — TechForce Ltd (DevOps, $750/day) and NovaBuild Inc (Backend, $700/day) — were engaged from 28 October under an 8-week SOW co-signed by the CTO and CFO. A third contractor (AWS migration specialist) was added in week three of November at an incremental cost of $6,500, with CTO approval. The combined November spend of $18,500 aligns with the CRM project record, which shows this as within the $50,000 total approved project budget, with Milestone 2 of 4 now complete and a January go-live expected. Note that the initial Slack estimate of ~$12,000 for November did not capture the additional $6,500 AWS specialist cost added mid-month, which accounts for the gap between estimate and actual. Remaining project spend of approximately $31,500 (i.e., $50,000 total budget less $18,500 spent) may be expected across December and January, though the exact schedule requires confirmation against the milestone plan.
Sources
- Gmail - 2024-11-16T18:15:00Z - gmail-contractor_spend-broad - SOW executed with TechForce Ltd (DevOps contractor) and NovaBuild Inc (Backend contractor). 8-week engagement from 28 Oct. Day rate schedule attached. CTO and CFO co-signed.
- Slack - 2024-11-16T12:15:00Z - slack-contractor_spend-broad - Platform migration kicked off — two senior contractors (DevOps + Backend) engaged for 8 weeks from 28 Oct. Day rates $750 and $700 per day respectively. Estimated Nov cost ~$12,000. CTO approved.
- Slack - 2024-11-16T12:45:00Z - slack-contractor_spend-narrow - Platform migration contractors working full-time alongside core team. Additional $6,500 for AWS migration specialist added week 3 — CTO approved extension.
- CRM - 2024-11-16T26:15:00Z - crm-contractor_spend-broad - Platform Migration Project (PLAT-001). Status: In Progress. Budget: $50,000 (total). Contractor spend Nov: $18,500 of $50K approved budget. Milestone 2 of 4 complete. On track for January go-live.

INSIGNIFICANT VARIANCES
The following lines are within acceptable tolerance thresholds and no detailed investigation was conducted. They are noted here for completeness.
- **Sales Salaries** — Variance of +$6,000 (+4.0%). Within acceptable range; no investigation required.
- **Benefits & Payroll Taxes** — Variance of +$4,000 (+8.0%). Likely moves broadly in line with the salary overages documented above; no independent investigation conducted.
